title: Thin film resonators
abstract: Thin film resonators are deployed in the port ducts of speakers, musical instruments, microphones, and other devices having an important auditory component, to reduce distortion and/or improve other sound qualities. Preferred resonators have a resonator sheet with a first air cavity, and a passageway in an edge of the resonator sheet that opens to the air cavity. The cavity is further defined by a cover sheet, and preferably also a base sheet. The sheets are preferably bonded together by an adhesive. Resonators can be deployed rolled up inside a duct, slightly curved, or laying substantially flat on a surface over which air is flowing. Resonators can be deployed during and/or post manufacturing. Cavities are preferably elliptically shaped, and deployed as substantially mirror image pairs. Openings to the cavities can be positioned on one or more edges of the resonators.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A thin film resonator comprising: a resonator sheet having a first fluid cavity; and a cover sheet that overlays the resonator sheet; and a first passageway in a first edge of the resonator sheet that opens to the fluid cavity, wherein the passageway is at least 1 mm long. 2. The resonator of claim 1 further comprising a base sheet disposed such that the resonator sheet is laminated between the cover sheet and the base sheet. 3. The resonator of claim 1 further comprising an adhesive disposed on at least one side of the resonator sheet. 4. The resonator of claim 1 wherein the resonator sheet comprises a flexible polymer. 5. The resonator of claim 1 wherein the resonator sheet has a nominal thickness of 125 Î¼Î¹Î· - 300 Î¼Î¹Î·. 6. The resonator of claim 1 wherein a top surface of the cover sheet has a series of wavy riblets. 7. The resonator of claim 1 wherein the first passageway has first and second ends, and narrows in width by at least 5% from the first end to the second end. 8. The resonator of claim 1 further comprising an opening to the fluid cavity from a side of the passageway. 9. The resonator of claim 1, wherein the resonator sheet has a second fluid cavity. 10. The resonator of claim 9, further comprising a second passageway that opens to a second fluid cavity, wherein the second passageway is at least 1 mm long. 11. The resonator of claim 10, wherein the second passageway is distanced from the first passageway by at least 5 mm. 12. The resonator of claim 10, wherein the first fluid cavity and first passageway are mirrored by the second fluid cavity and second passageway, respectively. 13. The resonator of claim 10, wherein the second passageway opens to an opposite edge of the resonator sheet. 14. The resonator of claim 1, wherein the first fluid cavity has a cross-sectional area of at least 300 mm . 15. The resonator of claim 14, wherein the resonator sheet has a second fluid cavity having a cross-sectional area that is within 10% of the cross-sectional area of the first fluid cavity. 16. The resonator of claim 1, wherein the first fluid cavity is round or elliptical. 17. The resonator of claim 1, wherein at the first passageway, the cover sheet extends beyond the resonator sheet by at least 1 mm. 18. The resonator claim 1 disposed within a speaker enclosure. 19. The resonator claim 1 disposed in an air passageway of a musical instrument. 20. The resonator claim 1 disposed in an air duct of an HVAC system. 21. The resonator claim 1 disposed on a blade of a fan.
